data.body: Consideration: [Reliability — Atomic merge orchestration] SC3 mandates that F ships in the same commit batch as the SC1 refactors (f3ce-beaf, f05a-0d49, d2c4-5515, dceb-2566) AND the boundary hook from story b1de-627f. This constraint is NOT enforced by the dependency graph alone. Recommended orchestration protocol: (1) land b1de-627f first (the hook + its allowlist with all six thin-wrapper filenames pre-enumerated since SC1 requires retained filenames); (2) land B, C, D, E (refactors) on a shared integration branch with F staged alongside — the final merge is a single squash-merge to main with a pre-merge combined-diff check running check-tickets-boundary.sh; (3) CI on the integration branch enforces that at no intermediate commit does the boundary hook find a violation that the same commit's doc sweep did not address. If the sprint orchestrator cannot support atomic batching, the alternative is to dissolve F — each of B/C/D/E owns the doc-reference updates for the script it refactors, and b1de-627f owns updates to CLAUDE.md and shared workflow docs. Either path must be chosen during implementation-plan. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
